Defendants Old Republic Title Company And Nick Shanta'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Nov-29-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. TIMUR BILIR, Esq. of Leonidou & Rosin (Tel: 650-691-2888) for plaintiff JKL Construction Services, Inc.;
Atty. JILL K. COHOE, Esq. of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-421-3400) for defendant Old Republic Title Company and Nick Shanta;
Atty. KAVITA SHARMA, Esq. of Versant Law Group, LLP (Tel: 415-429-6363) for defendants Beaubien Investment Group, LLC and William Francis Beaubien.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS NICK SHANTA AND OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY's Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Old Republic Title Co. and Nick Shanta's motion for good faith settlement determination is granted. Non-settling parties have not carried their burden of showing that the settlement is "so far 'out of the ballpark' as to be inconsistent" with equitable objectives. (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499-500.) Old Republic and its employee Shanta are escrow agents who believed they had no liability. They thus discussed a demurrer with plaintiffs - meeting and conferring that ripened into settlement discussions. That a settlement was reached before expensive pleading motions and discovery is laudable. And the settlement otherwise meets Tech-Bilt standards.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
